page: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v5 is an ARM64-based server instance powered by a 64-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance allocates 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-optimized 2.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. Additionally, the instance includes 2458 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and exhibits high read-only memory bandwidth, reaching 4384.56 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 177.23 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Software compilation via Clang and ray tracing workloads also perform in the top 25%. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by strong Passmark database scores and Redis SET performance of 84.83 million operations per second. Single-core performance and write memory bandwidth remain in the average tier.

Given its ARM64 architecture and high physical core density, the D64plds_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high memory-to-core ratios. The combination of 64 dedicated cores and 2458 GB of local storage makes it highly suitable for scale-out workloads, software development pipelines, containerized microservices, and high-throughput database operations. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB of memory per core ratio. It includes 2458 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks highlight strong multi-core execution, high read-only memory bandwidth, and excellent database throughput, with Redis SET operations reaching 84.83 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance and write memory bandwidth sit in the average tier. This hardware profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and database workloads, though the low memory-to-core ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-bound tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v5 is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and database throughput.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64plds_v5 is an ARM64 server featuring 64 Ampere cores, 128 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and substantial local storage for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB RAM and 2458 GB of local storage
Strong multi-core performance and high read-only memory bandwidth
Excellent database throughput, achieving 84.83 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for software compilation, multi-threaded applications, and scale-out databases
